summary: The diff implements the core role-based login system well, covering AuthContext, RoleSelectionLanding, RoleLoginPage, Navbar filtering, and App routing. However, the implementation uses an in-app state machine (landing/login/dashboard views) rather than actual URL routes (/login/student, /login/teacher, etc.), which partially misses the routing-based AC for REQ-1 and REQ-2. Route guarding for dashboard sub-routes is also not explicitly implemented via a router. All existing dashboard components remain intact.

missing_requirements.requirement: Each role card/button should navigate to a dedicated login route (e.g., /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, /login/parent)
missing_requirements.reason: The implementation uses an in-app state machine (pendingRole state) rather than actual URL routes. There is no React Router or URL-based routing to /login/student, /login/teacher, etc. The AC requires each role option to link to its own login route, but navigation is handled via onSelectRole callback and state, not URL changes.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement per-role login pages at /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, and /login/parent as distinct URL routes
missing_requirements.reason: The per-role login is implemented as a single RoleLoginPage component rendered conditionally by state (pendingRole), not as separate URL-routed pages. Visiting /login/student directly in the browser would not show the Student-themed login form because URL-based routing is absent.

missing_requirements.requirement: App router guards routes — unauthenticated users navigating to any dashboard route are redirected to the login landing page
missing_requirements.reason: There is no URL-based router in the diff (no React Router setup), so route guarding via redirect for direct URL access to dashboard routes is not implemented. The state-based approach handles in-app flow but does not protect URL routes from direct navigation.
